title: Careers
text: Talent Acquisition We welcome scientists, trainees, and collaborators who want to work at the interface of Alzheimer's disease genetics, human iPSC models, CRISPR genome editing, and multi-omics computational biology. Please complete the required fields and attach your cover letter, CV, and references. Major publication links are welcome but optional. Application received Thank you for applying to the TCW Lab. title: Welcome to TCW laboratory!
text: Research Interests Dr. Julia TCW received Ph.D. and A.M. in Molecular and Cellular Biology from Harvard University with research studies in induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) reprogramming in the Department of Stem Cell and Regenerative Biology. She then perused her postdoctoral research in the Department of Neuroscience, Ronald M. Loeb Center for Alzheimer’s Disease, Department of Genetics and Genomic Sciences at Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai, New York with a research focus of the development of iPSC models and study Alzheimer’s disease (AD)

title: Lab Members
text: Principal Investigator Julia TCW, Ph.D. Julia TCW is a Principal Investigator at the TCW laboratory at Boston University (primary appointment at Pharmacology and Experimental Therapeutics). She received a Ph.D. and A.M. in Molecular and Cellular Biology from Harvard University with a Biophysics training background and research studies in induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) reprogramming in the Department of Stem Cell and Regenerative Biology. She then perused her postdoctoral research in the Department of Neuroscience, Ronald M. Loeb Center for Alzheime

title: Our newest publication is in Cell!
text: June 23, 2022 We are excited to share that “Cholesterol and matrisome pathways dysregulated in astrocytes and microglia” is published in Molecular Cell. Here, we used hiPSCs, post-mortem human brain and mouse models to study about the strongest AD risk factor, APOE4. Using these models, Dr. TCW and colleagues found that there are more than just APOE4 mutation, which is APOE nearby genetic region called haplotype to increase disease risk, and the mechanistic defects due to APOE4 are unique to human cells. title: Deepti Graduation
text: July 12, 2024 Deepti Murthy, a diligent dry lab student that specialized in single-cell sequencing analysis, has graduated from college and departs for San Diego to pursue a Ph.D. in bioinformatics. It has been a pleasure to have Deepti as a member of the lab and we all look forward to witness her exciting work in the future.

title: Emmanuel’s Graduation
text: June 10, 2025 Emmanuel Mekasha, our 2nd undergraduate student graduating from the TCW LAB. CONGRATULATIONS, Emmanuel! We are very proud of you!

title: First Lab Meeting
text: February 19, 2022 We had our first team meeting virtually this Monday!

title: Juao becomes recipient of T32 Grant!
text: July 6, 2022 PhD Student Juao-Guilherme Rosa was awarded the NIGMS T32 BioMolecular Pharmacology grant. We're truly excited for him and wish him for more such achievements in the future.

title: Juao-Guilherme Rosa won the Trainee Professional Development Award
text: August 2, 2024 Juao's exciting work has been recognized by the Society for Neuroscience (SfN) for scientific excellence and has won the travel grant for attending Neuroscience 2024 in Chicago. Congratulations

title: Lu Qian is selected in the Neurophotonics Research Traineeship Program (NRT)
text: May 22, 2024 PhD student Lu Qian was selected as a trainee in the Neurophotonics Research Traineeship Program (NRT), which is a university-wide community with focused professional development and graduate training in neurophotonics . Congratulations!

title: Research Projects
text: Computational AD Genomics Using a computational approach, we are working to better characterize the effects of genetics on Alzheimer's disease molecular phenotypes. As part of a multi-lab collaboration through the Functional Genomics Consortium and xQTL studies in the Alzheimer's Disease Sequencing Project, we integrate large-scale genetic, epigenetic, transcriptomic, proteomic, and metabolomic datasets to identify molecular pathways associated with AD risk. These analyses help prioritize mechanisms for follow-up in iPSC-derived CNS cell models and suppo

title: Research News
text: Do Lipids Lubricate ApoE's Part in Alzheimer Mechanisms? 05 November 2021, Alzforum APOE4 Glia Fumble Lipid Handling APOE is an apolipoprotein, tasked with ferrying cholesterol and other lipids between cells. In the brain, it is produced mainly by astrocytes and activated microglia. Recent work by Julia TCW and Alison Goate at Icahn School of Medicine, Mount Sinai, New York, found the E4 allele disrupts the lipid balance in astrocytes and microglia derived from human iPSCs, boosting cholesterol production while hobbling its export ( Jul 2018 news ; Apr 2
